title: INTENT_IR — NLQ-to-MBQL Intermediate Representation
description: Schema for the intermediate representation produced by the NLQ translator when converting natural language queries to MBQL. Each INTENT_IR captures the translator's interpretation of the user's question before MBQL generation.

nlq.description: The original natural language query exactly as submitted

intent.description: Classified intent type from the NLQ translator

source.description: Target data source for the query

field.description: The field name to filter on (dotted path for nested fields)

value.description: The value to compare against. Type depends on field and operator.

projection.description: Fields to return in results. Use '*' for all fields. Use 'COUNT(*)' for count aggregation.

group_by.description: Field to group results by, if aggregating

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

confidence.description: Translator's confidence in the intent classification (0.0-1.0)

disambiguation_notes.description: If confidence < 0.85, notes explaining what was ambiguous

invariant_id.description: For INVARIANT intent: the invariant ID to check
